title: Genomics Researcher
description: Genomics researchers study the structure, function, evolution, and mapping of genomes. They analyze DNA, RNA, and other biological molecules to understand genetic variations, disease mechanisms, and develop new diagnostic and therapeutic strategies. In India, this field is crucial for addressing public health challenges and improving agricultural practices.
content: Genomics researchers in India work in diverse settings, including universities, research institutes (like CSIR-IGIB, CCMB), pharmaceutical companies, and agricultural research centers. They use advanced technologies like next-generation sequencing, CRISPR-Cas9, and bioinformatics to analyze large datasets and conduct experiments. The work involves designing experiments, analyzing data, writing research papers, and presenting findings at conferences.

financialFeasibility.note: Higher education, including a Master's or PhD, is essential. While government funding and scholarships are available, access can be competitive. Financial stability helps in pursuing uninterrupted research and attending conferences.

sectorScore.remarks: Both government-funded research institutions and private pharmaceutical/biotech companies offer opportunities. Government jobs provide stability, while the private sector may offer higher salaries and faster career growth.

relatedCareers.reason: Analyzes large-scale genomic data using computational tools.
relatedCareers.title: Bioinformatician

relatedCareers.reason: Provides guidance and support to individuals and families affected by genetic disorders.
relatedCareers.title: Genetic Counselor

relatedCareers.reason: Studies the structure and function of biological molecules, including DNA and RNA.
relatedCareers.title: Molecular Biologist

genderDistribution.note: The field is becoming increasingly gender-balanced, with more women pursuing careers in STEM and genomics.
